title: Help & FAQ
sections.intro.title: Getting started
sections.intro.what.a: LeetVault is a desktop app to save, organise and review the LeetCode problems you solve. You can record your solution, notes and the algorithmic pattern, and use spaced repetition so you don't forget what you have learnt.
sections.intro.manual.q: How do I add a problem manually?
sections.intro.manual.a: Open the Problems tab and click the ＋ New button. Fill in number, title, difficulty, pattern and your solution. Click Save.
sections.intro.fromLeetcode.q: How do I add a problem from LeetCode automatically?
sections.intro.fromLeetcode.a: Install the bundled Chrome extension (see the Extension section below). With the extension enabled, open any problem on leetcode.com and click the LeetVault icon in the browser bar. The extension picks up the number, title, difficulty and your editor code automatically.
sections.problems.title: Problems tab
sections.problems.filter.q: How do I search or filter my problems?
sections.problems.filter.a: At the top you'll find a free-text search (matches title and notes), a difficulty filter (Easy / Medium / Hard) and a status filter (Solved / In Progress / To Review).
sections.problems.statuses.a:
• Solved — you finished it and it's ready for the review cycle.
• In Progress — half-done or something you want to come back to.
• To Review — flagged for a refresh before an interview.
sections.problems.editDelete.q: Can I edit or delete a problem?
sections.problems.editDelete.a: Yes. Click the pencil icon to open the edit form, or the trash can to delete it.
sections.review.title: Review tab (spaced repetition)
sections.review.what.a: It's a research-backed memorisation technique. LeetVault uses the SM-2 algorithm: the better you remember a problem, the more days pass before it shows up again — so you spend time only on what you actually need to revisit.
sections.review.howSession.q: How does a review session work?
sections.review.howSession.a:
The Review tab shows problems whose review date has arrived. Try to solve the problem, then rate your recall with one of the 5 buttons:
• Blackout — total blank → back in 1 day
• Hard — recalled with great effort → 2 days
• Good — recalled with some difficulty → 3–7 days
• Easy — recalled without trouble → 7–14 days
• Perfect — recalled as if you'd done it yesterday → 30+ days
sections.review.whenAdded.q: When is a problem added to the review cycle?
sections.review.whenAdded.a: Automatically once its status becomes Solved. The first review appears the next day.
sections.stats.title: Stats tab
sections.stats.what.q: What does the Stats tab show?
sections.stats.what.a:
• Distribution by difficulty (pie chart).
• Top 10 most-practised patterns (bar chart).
• Activity heatmap: a calendar of the days you solved problems, GitHub-style. Hover any cell to see how many problems you solved that day.
sections.roadmap.title: Roadmap tab
sections.roadmap.what.a: A visual map of the algorithmic patterns that matter most for technical interviews (Two Pointers, Sliding Window, BFS/DFS, Dynamic Programming, etc.). It shows how many problems you've solved for each pattern and your overall progress.
sections.roadmap.filter.q: Can I filter problems by pattern from the Roadmap?
sections.roadmap.filter.a: Yes. Click any pattern in the Roadmap and the Problems tab opens automatically filtered by that pattern.
sections.extension.title: Chrome extension
sections.extension.install.q: How do I install the extension?
sections.extension.install.a:
1. Open Chrome and go to chrome://extensions
2. Enable Developer mode (top-right corner)
3. Click "Load unpacked"
4. Select the leetcode_extension folder bundled with LeetVault (click "Open extension folder" to find it)
5. The extension will appear in your Chrome toolbar.
sections.extension.requires.q: Does the extension work if LeetVault isn't running?
sections.extension.requires.a: No. The extension talks to the desktop app through a local server (port 7842). LeetVault must be open and running to save problems from Chrome.
sections.extension.data.q: What data does the extension capture?
sections.extension.data.a: Only the data of the problem you're viewing: number, title, difficulty, tags and the code in LeetCode's Monaco editor. It doesn't send anything to any external server — everything goes straight to your local app.
sections.ai.title: AI hint (Groq)
sections.ai.what.a: An optional extension feature. When you click "AI hint" in the extension popup, the problem title and your current code are sent to the (free) Groq API and you get back a 2-3 sentence hint about the strategy or pattern to use — no full solution.
sections.ai.setup.q: How do I get and configure my Groq API key?
sections.ai.setup.a:
1. Go to console.groq.com and create a free account
2. In the dashboard, open "API Keys" → "Create API Key"
3. Copy the key (starts with gsk_...)
4. Open the extension on leetcode.com → click "AI hint"
5. The first time it'll ask for the key → paste it and click Save
The key is stored only in your browser (chrome.storage.local). LeetVault never sees or stores it.
sections.ai.cost.a: Groq's free tier is more than enough for personal use: thousands of requests a month. The model used (Llama 3.1 8B) is very fast and efficient.
sections.ai.error401.q: The AI hint returns error 401 — what should I do?
sections.ai.error401.a: Your API key isn't valid or has expired. The extension wipes the saved key automatically and prompts for a new one. Go to console.groq.com to generate a new key.
sections.privacy.title: Privacy & data
sections.privacy.location.a: Everything is stored locally on your machine in a SQLite database called leetcode.db. The path depends on your OS (Windows: %APPDATA%\LeetVault; macOS: ~/Library/Application Support/LeetVault; Linux: ~/.config/LeetVault). You can back it up by copying that file.
sections.privacy.network.q: Does LeetVault send anything to the internet?
sections.privacy.network.a: Only if you use the AI hint — in which case the problem title and your code are sent to the Groq API using your own key. Nothing else. The main app is 100% offline.
sections.privacy.backup.q: How do I back up my problems?
sections.privacy.backup.a: Copy the leetcode.db file from the path above wherever you want. To restore, paste it back into the same folder (close LeetVault first).
